name: State of Indie Web 2025
title: Annual report
summary: A long-form annual community survey report — methodology, demographic charts, tool rankings, sentiment scales, and an editor's takeaways.
setup_questions: What community or audience is this survey for?
How many people responded, and when did the survey run?
What are the 3–4 headline questions or findings you want to feature?
What tools, platforms, or options should appear in the ranking charts?
photo_terms: person coding at desk dual monitors
sections: Intro + methodology (how many responded, how they were sampled, dates the survey ran)
Demographics: years of experience, company size, country, role (bar and donut charts)
Tools & frameworks ranking with usage vs satisfaction bars
Hosting & deployment platform popularity horizontal bar race
Biggest pain points ranked list with vote counts
Sentiment: 'how do you feel about the web platform' Likert-scale diverging bars
Key takeaways / editor's commentary and 'what changed since last year'
Appendix: full respondent counts and a 'share this report' footer
based_on: An annual community survey results writeup in the lineage of State of JS and the Stack Overflow Developer Survey — a long scrollable report of charts, rankings, and commentary published by a developer community (here, indie web developers and solo founders).
